_comment: Claim-fidelity spec for the retro skill: does a real `/charness:retro` run honor its central claim — route to its references at the point of need? retro has no planner that emits required reads; RCF is derived from the SKILL.md workflow plus script-resolution analysis (lens 3/7). The prior RCF of four was over-broad; three of the four dissolve under lens 7 because deterministic surfaces (SKILL.md body + scaffold_retro_artifact.py + resolve_adapter.py) brief the judge: mode-guide.md (SKILL.md step 1 inlines the session/weekly selection rule and the Auto Selection order, step 2 inlines the per-mode evidence bias) is engage-always but not a floor (claimRole skill-inlined-context); section-guide.md (the core section list is inlined in SKILL.md step 3 + Output Shape AND emitted by scaffold_retro_artifact.py's template; the doc's unique depth — claim-strength tags, gate-baseline-runtime rule — is conditional within a retro) is engage-always but not a floor (claimRole skill-and-scaffold-inlined-context); adapter-contract.md is resolved by the mandatory resolve_adapter.py bootstrap and the explainer is read only on adapter repair, so it is on-demand (conditional). trigger-and-persistence.md stays engage-always-not-RCF (persist_retro_artifact.py owns the persistence mechanism and SKILL.md step 5 inlines the mandatory Persisted: yes/no rule; the trigger taxonomy is the doc's conditional remainder). The single engage-always floor is expert-lens.md: the Expert Counterfactual Rule mandates 'every retro includes at least one counterfactual lens' and routes 'the lens patterns, named-expert examples, and sub-agent flow live in references/expert-lens.md' at the point of need; the scaffold emits only a '## Expert Counterfactuals — TODO a lens' stub, so to fill the mandatory section the run is forced to open expert-lens.md for the default pattern (one domain lens + one decision-quality lens), the named-expert examples, and the sub-agent flow — content neither the scaffold nor the SKILL.md body inlines. The prompt is pinned to a representative session retro of concrete recent work (bare /charness:retro can self-activate on recent git/handoff state, but a fixture needs a deterministic, executable work unit — lens 2 + lens 8) so the run reaches the mandatory counterfactual where expert-lens.md is forced; it names neither the reference file nor the lens patterns (ungameable). retro persists its artifact in-repo via persist_retro_artifact.py (sandboxed in a capture), not an external write, so no lens-5 external non-claim applies. Fixture-only: expert-lens.md is already routed at the point of need and the other docs are appropriately inlined/scaffolded/script-resolved — no skill defect. declaredReferences is the full skill-local set (coverage); shared refs (bootstrap-resolution.md, retro-issue-destination-split.md, rca-ledger-append.md) are not skill-local so they are not declared. referenceEngagement uses the engage-always/on-demand/gate-sufficient axis from the methodology contract. thresholds omitted until a baseline capture sets a budget.
prompt: /charness:retro 방금 끝낸 claim-fidelity 픽스처 보정 작업을 session retro로 review해줘 — Context·Waste·Critical Decisions를 정리하고, 다음 세션을 바꿀 counterfactual lens(named expert 또는 direct) 최소 하나와 workflow/capability/memory별 Next Improvements를 도출한 뒤, durable home이 있으면 persist해줘.

adapter-contract.md.rationale: resolve_adapter.py resolves the adapter on every bootstrap, but the explainer doc is read only 'if the adapter is invalid, repair it using references/adapter-contract.md' — a representative session retro against a valid adapter never opens it, so the prior engage-always-RCF tag was a lens-1/3 over-classification.
adapter-contract.md.trigger: When the adapter is invalid and must be repaired, or a weekly/metrics-heavy retro needs adapter-defined evidence/metrics fields.

expert-lens.md.rationale: The single engage-always floor. The Expert Counterfactual Rule mandates 'every retro includes at least one counterfactual lens' and routes 'the lens patterns, named-expert examples, and sub-agent flow live in references/expert-lens.md' at the point of need; the scaffold emits only a '## Expert Counterfactuals — TODO a lens' stub, so to fill the mandatory section the run is forced to open this doc for content neither the scaffold nor SKILL.md inlines.

mode-guide.md.rationale: Every run selects a mode (step 1), but SKILL.md step 1 inlines the session/weekly selection rule and the Auto Selection order and step 2 inlines the per-mode evidence bias — the run engages mode selection from the workflow, not by opening this doc: engage-always yet not an RCF floor (lens 7, skill-inlined).

phase-aware-efficiency.md.rationale: Step 3 conditionally routes here only 'for token, tool-call, broad exploration, or efficiency claims'; it applies the phase check to avoid mislabeling broad exploration as waste, but is not needed in retros with no such claims.
phase-aware-efficiency.md.trigger: When a retro discusses token pressure, tool-call volume, repeated file reads, broad exploration, or efficiency claims during waste analysis.

prepare-packet.md.rationale: Step 2 routes here only 'if the adapter declares packet_sections', preventing rediscovery of changed surfaces after work has moved on; repos without packet_sections do not read this.
prepare-packet.md.trigger: When the retro-adapter declares packet_sections and the retro needs context on changed surfaces or deferred work before turning a slice into lessons.

section-guide.md.rationale: Every run writes the core sections, but the section list is inlined in SKILL.md step 3 + Output Shape AND emitted by scaffold_retro_artifact.py's template; the doc's unique depth (claim-strength tags strong/moderate/weak/contested, the gate-baseline-runtime rule, per-decision fields) is conditional within a retro, so the run engages the section structure from the workflow + scaffold rather than being forced to open this doc: engage-always yet not an RCF floor (lens 7, skill-and-scaffold-inlined).

trigger-and-persistence.md.rationale: persist_retro_artifact.py owns the persistence mechanism and SKILL.md step 5 inlines the mandatory 'Persisted: yes: <path> / Persisted: no: <reason>' rule; the doc's auto-trigger/skip taxonomy is its conditional remainder, so the run satisfies the persistence claim from the script + inlined rule, not by opening this doc: engage-always yet not an RCF floor.

waste-sibling-scan.md.rationale: Step 4 conditionally routes here when 'a lesson names a transferable waste pattern', adding an opt-in Sibling Search section; narrowly local waste uses the short-circuit instead.
waste-sibling-scan.md.trigger: When a lesson names a transferable waste pattern in step 4 (the four-axis scan and section-gated validator).

weekly-trends.md.rationale: Applicable only in weekly mode for the Trends vs Last Retro section and closeout-telemetry mining; session retros do not consult this reference.
weekly-trends.md.trigger: When the mode is weekly and the retro needs to compare against the last durable weekly retro or mine closeout-telemetry trends.
